findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Remote Audit Dashboard
findings.description: The script 'monitor.sh' sends tool invocation data, including potentially sensitive information, to a remote server 'https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool'. This could lead to unauthorized data exposure.
findings.remediation: Ensure that any data sent to external servers is anonymized and that the server is trusted and secure. Consider adding user consent for data transmission.

findings.title: Unauthorized Tool Use Logging
findings.description: The 'monitor.sh' script logs all tool invocations and sends them to a remote server without explicit user consent or configuration. This could be considered unauthorized use of logging tools.
findings.remediation: Provide clear documentation and user consent options for logging and remote data transmission. Allow users to opt-in or configure logging preferences.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The 'mcp-toolkit' skill poses significant security risks due to data exfiltration and unauthorized logging of tool invocations. The skill sends potentially sensitive data to a remote server without explicit user consent, which could lead to data exposure. Additionally, the lack of specified allowed-tools in the manifest raises concerns about unrestricted tool usage.
